name: Retention policy configuration
description: Expose a configurable retention policy (how long to keep non-preserved routes before deletion) via env var at startup and DB-backed override at runtime. The cleanup-worker feature consumes this; this feature only plumbs the configuration.
acceptance_criteria: New env var RETENTION_DAYS (integer, default 0 which means 'never delete'); documented in CLAUDE.md and README.md

sql/migrations/005_retention_settings.up.sql creates a settings table (key TEXT PK, value TEXT, updated_at) -- simple KV for operator-configurable runtime settings; seeds 'retention_days' from the env var on first boot if the row does not exist
internal/config loads RETENTION_DAYS from env; a new internal/settings package reads/writes the settings table with methods GetInt(ctx, key) (int, error) and SetInt(ctx, key string, value int) error
At cleanup time, effective retention is settings['retention_days'] with env var as fallback; 0 always means 'never delete'
PUT /v1/settings/retention with { retention_days: int } updates the setting (JWT-authed); value must be 0 or a positive integer, else 400
internal/settings/settings_test.go and internal/api/*_test.go cover the API + DB path
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
notes: Implemented in commits 522bfa1, 279713f, 914d5c6, a742e65. Settings table is a simple KV store seeded from RETENTION_DAYS at boot via InsertSettingIfMissing. internal/settings.Store exposes GetInt/SetInt plus a RetentionDays helper that falls back to the env value when the row is missing. Settings handler wired into the authed /v1 group. go test, go vet, go build all clean; smoke.sh frontend lint/typecheck failures are pre-existing and unrelated (script tries to run eslint/tsc from repo root instead of web/).
